description: Master list of SSA events. All SSA modules should emit events using the common envelope { "type", "ts", "source", "data" }. This catalog is used by the automation runtime, import stack, session engines, and (optionally) the Hub exporter when familyFundMode=true.

envelope.type: string (required) — must match one of events[].id below
envelope.ts: string (required, ISO 8601) — when the event was emitted
envelope.source: string (required) — emitter name or module path, e.g. "features/import/ImportService"

events.description: Periodic signal that SSA is alive and the event bus is working.

dataShape.features: array of strings — enabled feature flags

events.description: Emitted when a subsystem throws or returns an error that should be visible to automation/logging.

dataShape.context: object — anything useful, e.g. incoming payload

events.description: A user changed SSA preferences — import behavior, guards, dashboards, or scheduling rules.

dataShape.source: string — UI component or API caller

events.description: A user saved a session, schedule, or plan as a favorite to reuse later.

dataShape.favoriteType: string — e.g. meal-session, cleaning-session, garden-plan, import-template
dataShape.entityRef: object — pointer to the origin entity

events.description: Raw import has arrived from any source (browser share, iOS shortcut, bookmarklet, file upload, connected site).

dataShape.sourceKind: string — e.g. ios.shortcut, web.share, bookmarklet, csv.upload, pdf.upload
dataShape.hint: string | null — user/domain hint about data type, e.g. 'garden/seed', 'cleaning/routine'

events.description: Import was successfully normalized into SSA’s internal structure (recipe, cleaning, garden, animal, storehouse).

dataShape.normalizedType: string — recipe | cleaning-plan | garden-plan | animal-plan | storehouse-plan | howto

events.description: High-level signal for listeners: an import has been parsed and is ready to be linked to inventory/storehouse and to generate sessions.

dataShape.parsedType: string — recipe | cleaning | garden | animal | storehouse | preservation | howto
dataShape.contextIntelId: string | null — reference to stored context intelligence record

events.description: Import could not be parsed or normalized. Good place for UI to show error and for retry logic.
Context intelligence has been derived from an import (ingredients, methods, equipment, seasonality, cuisine).

dataShape.intelType: string — ingredients | methods | equipment | seasonality | cuisine

events.description: Inventory has been updated — quantities adjusted, new item added, or metadata changed.

dataShape.changeType: string — add | adjust | remove | consume | produce
dataShape.delta: number | object — numeric diff or full diff

events.description: Inventory rule engine detected a shortage and recommends a path: garden → animal → storehouse → grocery.
A cooking/meal session has been generated from a recipe or import.

dataShape.inventoryLinks: array — inventory item ids or suggested subs
dataShape.schedule: object — when SSA thinks it should be cooked

events.description: A meal was actually cooked/executed; inventory should already be decremented by the session engine.
A cleaning/decluttering session has been generated from an import (Pinterest routine, blog routine, YT how-to) or from templates.
Cleaning session marked as done — helpful for household analytics and for exporting to Hub if needed.
A garden plan/work session was created from import (seed packet) or from inventory shortage signals.

dataShape.tasks: array — sow, transplant, water, fertilize, harvest

events.description: Garden harvest was logged, typically feeding into inventory/storehouse and triggering preservation.
Animal acquisition/care/butchery plan was generated from an import or storehouse shortage.

dataShape.actions: array — acquire, feed, breed, butcher, process

events.description: An animal butchery event was logged; this can produce multiple inventory/storehouse items.
A preservation session (canning, drying, fermenting, freezing, curing, distilling) has completed and updated inventory/storehouse.

dataShape.method: string — can, dehydrate, ferment, freeze, cure, distill

events.description: Any module can ask the automation runtime to schedule a session/plan; the runtime will apply guards (Sabbath, quiet hours, weather).
Automation runtime accepted a request and turned it into a task.
Automation runtime executed a scheduled task.

dataShape.guard: string | null — which guard prevented execution (if any)

events.description: SSA has familyFundMode=true and is requesting to export a payload to the Hub.

dataShape.eventRef: string — original event id that triggered this export

events.description: Hub export was delivered successfully.
Hub export failed; SSA should fail silently for the user, but we keep telemetry.

notes: 1. All emitters should use the common envelope: { type, ts: new Date().toISOString(), source, data }.
2. If a module changes household data (inventory, storehouse, sessions), it SHOULD check featureFlags.familyFundMode and, if true, emit hub.export.requested with the original event as payload.
3. New domains should add their events here so the automation runtime and UI dashboards can discover them dynamically.
4. This file is intentionally flat JSON so it can be loaded in both browser and service-worker contexts.
5. If you still want schema validation, create a local file at src/schemas/eventCatalog.schema.json and point $schema to "./src/schemas/eventCatalog.schema.json".
